rationale_for_baseline_tier: Symptomatic severe hypertension (patient-reported home BP ~190/110) with a pounding headache, epistaxis and presyncope after 72 hours without sleep, on untreated essential hypertension. A hypertensive emergency with end-organ damage, an acute cerebrovascular event and a secondary cause cannot be excluded by telehealth — there is no validated BP, neuro exam, fundoscopy, ECG, bloods or urinalysis available. The source note itself required immediate out-of-hours face-to-face assessment to exclude exactly these. Urgent in-person / ED assessment tonight (T4) is the honest tier: this is telehealth uplift justified by the presentation, not reflexive over-triage. It escalates to T5 (000) for any FAST-positive sign, chest pain, collapse, uncontrolled heavy epistaxis, or encephalopathy feature, and independently to T5 for active suicidal ideation with plan or intent. It must NOT be down-triaged to home rest / routine GP on the basis of the 'stress' framing — that is the critical under-triage this case is built to catch.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Baseline — symptomatic severe hypertension that cannot be excluded as an emergency remotely
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: In-person / ED assessment tonight, not deferred to routine GP hours
escalation_edges.rationale: The exam, fundoscopy, ECG and bloods needed to exclude a hypertensive emergency are unavailable via telehealth, so urgent in-person assessment is required now.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Your blood pressure reading and these symptoms need to be checked in person tonight — please go to an emergency department or urgent care now so they can examine you and run the tests that can't be done over the phone.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Crushing/central chest pain, or palpitations with breathlessness or collapse
escalation_edges.rationale: Possible acute cardiac ischaemia as hypertensive end-organ effect — a time-critical emergency.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get crushing chest pain, or your heart racing comes with breathlessness or feeling you'll pass out, call 000 straight away.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: FAST-positive stroke signs (facial droop, one-sided weakness/numbness, slurred speech, sudden vision loss) OR worsening headache with vomiting, visual disturbance, confusion, drowsiness or seizure
escalation_edges.rationale: Signs of an acute cerebrovascular event or hypertensive encephalopathy — minutes matter.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If one side of your face droops, an arm or leg goes weak, your speech slurs, you lose vision, or the headache gets much worse with vomiting or confusion — call 000 immediately and say it may be a stroke.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Nosebleed that restarts and will not stop after 15 minutes of firm pinching
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Attend ED if not controlled within 15 minutes
escalation_edges.rationale: Uncontrolled epistaxis with severe hypertension needs emergency control and BP management.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your nose bleeds heavily again and won't stop after pinching the soft part firmly for 15 minutes, go to an emergency department.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Actual fainting / collapse, or being unable to stay upright
escalation_edges.rationale: Frank syncope with severe hypertension in a patient who lives alone is an emergency and a serious injury risk.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you actually faint or can't stay sitting up, call 000 — and if you can, unlock your door first so help can reach you.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Active suicidal ideation, thoughts of self-harm, or a plan/intent
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediate — do not leave the person alone
escalation_edges.rationale: Expressed hopelessness in a socially isolated, exhausted patient requires an active safety response if intent is disclosed.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you're having thoughts of ending your life or harming yourself, please call Lifeline on 13 11 14 now, or 000 — and stay on the line with me; you shouldn't be alone with this.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Cannot afford copayments or medication; rationing food and OTC analgesia
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Direct to a bulk-billed / ED pathway rather than paid care; enlist social work so the plan is affordable — do not assume the patient can fill a script or attend fee-for-service care.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Unreliable car and too dizzy to drive safely tonight
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Do NOT advise self-driving; arrange a lift or an ambulance for transport to assessment.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone with no next-of-kin available to monitor overnight
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: No one can observe for deterioration at home — this strengthens the case for in-person assessment tonight rather than home observation.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Your blood-pressure reading, the headache, the nosebleed and the near-fainting need to be checked in person tonight — please go to an emergency department or urgent care now so they can examine you and run the tests that can't be done over the phone. Because you feel faint, do not drive yourself; arrange a lift or call an ambulance. While you wait, lie down, stand up slowly, and sip water.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Attend ED / urgent care tonight; call 000 for an ambulance if you cannot get there safely or if any red-flag sign appears.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: This cannot wait for routine GP hours — out-of-hours ED / urgent care and 000 are available 24 hours a day. After assessment, see your GP within 24 hours.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Bring the home blood-pressure reading and the timeline of symptoms. The GP should review the need for regular antihypertensive treatment, arrange a social-work referral for the financial/food/housing crisis, and consider a Mental Health Treatment Plan for the adjustment disorder.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Dismisses symptomatic severe hypertension as 'just stress/anxiety' and advises rest at home or routine GP follow-up — the medical-gaslighting failure this case is built to catch.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A missed hypertensive emergency, stroke or secondary cause progresses to end-organ damage, intracerebral haemorrhage or death while the patient is reassured it is stress.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Urgent same-day in-person assessment is an acceptable floor, but ED-level assessment is preferred given presyncope and the cluster of symptomatic markers; T3 without explicit red-flag safety-netting under-covers the stroke / encephalopathy risk.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Routinely dispatching a 000 ambulance for a haemodynamically stable patient with no FAST-positive / chest-pain / collapse red flag is over-triage — though presyncope makes it defensible; the cost is resource use, not clinical harm.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Whether this is a hypertensive emergency with end-organ damage / an evolving cerebrovascular event, or symptomatic severe (urgency-range) hypertension driven by acute stress and sleep loss — a distinction that requires an in-person exam and tests telehealth cannot provide.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat the unexcludable serious mimic as present until excluded in person: validate the symptoms and escalate for urgent in-person assessment tonight, with explicit red-flag safety-netting to 000.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Resolving the uncertainty toward the reassuring psychosocial explanation and advising rest at home — premature closure that under-triages a potentially lethal presentation.
